title: cytisus supinus var. jankae
intro: Cytisus supinus var. jankae, also known as Jankae's kidney vetch or a variety of the Kidney Vetch, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects, making it useful in treating urinary tract infections and kidney stones. The health benefits of Cytisus supinus var. jankae include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote overall urinary system health. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed orally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
